channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about activist Mahmoud Khalil and his attorneys' plan to appeal his deportation ruling
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge has ruled that a Palestinian Columbia University graduate student who participated in protests against Israel can be deported. Mahmoud Khalil's attorneys said they will appeal Friday's ruling.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to target chronic disease in US tribes. A key program to do that was gutted
item.description: CHANDLER, Ariz. (AP) — Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. spent time in tribal communities in Arizona and New Mexico this week highlighting ways they are trying to prevent chronic disease among Native Americans and Alaska Natives, something he has said is one of his top priorities.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE, GRAHAM LEE BREWER and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Strict Idaho abortion ban loosened by judge's ruling on medical exemptions
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An abortion in Idaho is not prohibited if pregnancy complications could cause a woman's death, even if that death “is neither imminent nor assured,” a state judge said Friday in a ruling that loosens one of the strictest abortion bans in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington, DC, faces budget crisis after House leaves $1.1 billion hole in city coffers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the House of Representatives adjourned for a two-week recess Thursday, it left Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump undergoes his annual physical after years of reluctance to share medical information
item.description: Donald Trump had his annual physical on Friday, a check-up that may give the public its first details in years about the health of a man who, in January, became the oldest in U.S. history to be sworn in as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump administration offers no details on mistakenly deported man after court ruling
item.description: A federal judge on Friday lambasted a government lawyer who couldn’t explain what, if anything, President Donald Trump’s administration has done to arrange for the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Picture of Trump after the assassination attempt displaces Obama portrait at the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Friday hung a painting of President Donald Trump depicting a moment after an assassination attempt against him last summer — bumping the official portrait of one of his predecessors, Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Universal announces UK theme park amid 'epic' growth of parks business
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences is expanding its global theme park footprint, confirming a new theme park will be built in the U.K. -- the latest in a trio of ambitious projects for the company.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: NC police release new photo of missing girl Madalina Cojocari on her 14th birthday
item.description: North Carolina police have released a new age-progression photo of missing Cornelius girl Madalina Cojocari, who was 11 when she was last seen getting off a school bus in November 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: The Trump administration directed the Social Security Administration to place more than 6,300 migrants on its list for tracking dead people, blocking their ability to work in a move it hopes will encourage them to “self-deport.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Fatal NYC helicopter crash prompts GOP lawmaker to call for ending popular tourist flights
item.description: A GOP lawmaker has called for ending New York City helicopter tour flights in the wake of Thursday's deadly tragedy on the Hudson River.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense files motion to preclude death penalty in his federal case
item.description: Luigi Mangione's defense team is asking a federal judge to throw out the possibility of the death penalty in the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Feds say agents went to LA schools to do welfare check on migrant children
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Homeland Security officers visited two Los Angeles public elementary schools this week to do a welfare check on migrant children, not for immigration enforcement, the department said Friday. The officers were denied access by both principals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida universities to deputize campus police for immigration enforcement
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The police departments of at least three public universities in Florida are seeking agreements with the federal government to carry out immigration enforcement on campus.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social Security 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ says whereabouts of Maryland immigrant wrongly deported to El Salvador are unknown
item.description: The DOJ says the whereabouts of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland-based Salvadoran immigrant wrongfully deported to an El Salvador last month, are unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department tasked with enforcing Trump's transgender sports ban in Maine
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine officials said Friday the state will not comply with a ban on transgender athletes in high school sports in the wake of a Trump administration finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing the students to participate. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: A Louisiana immigration judge says Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be deported as a national security risk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration refers Maine to DOJ over transgender athletes
item.description: The Department of Education on Friday referred a Title IX investigation into Maine schools to the Justice Department after the state failed to reach a resolution with the Trump administration over a finding that it violated federal anti-discrimination law by allowing transgender students to particip
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security Administration 'will be using X to communicate' moving forward
item.description: The shift comes as communications staff at the agency has dwindled due to reassignments in front-facing roles at field offices across the country.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Plane crash near Florida highway kills 3 on board, injures 1 on the ground
item.description: A spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said the plane, a Cessna 310, crashed at around 10:20 a.m. after departing from the nearby airport.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amateur golfer urinates in creek at Masters Tournament, gets round of applause
item.description: U.S. Amateur champion Jose Luis Ballester had to take a different kind of relief Thursday at the Masters, just not the kind found in the Rules of Golf.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, WGN 9

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Divers search for parts after NYC helicopter crash killed a family of five and the pilot, a Navy vet
item.description: Police and fire department divers are searching for the main and rear rotors of a sightseeing helicopter that broke apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River between New York City and New Jersey, killing six people.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Jennifer Peltz And Ted Shaffrey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Stocks climb but the dollar sinks as Wall Street closes a chaotic week
item.description: U.S. stocks are climbing in shaky trading Friday as President Donald Trump’s trade war with China escalates further and U.S. households get more worried about it.
item.dc:creator: Stan Choe, Fox 5

item.title: Trump racks up another win on illegal immigration and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Los Angeles woman arrested in Russia has been freed in a prisoner swap. Here's what we know
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Moscow has freed a Russian American convicted of treason in exchange for a Russian German man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S. The prisoner swap was completed Thursday and Ksenia Karelina was "on a plane back home to the United States,” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, Fox 5
